stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model as a trending item on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function.
Relevant technologies mentioned include transformers and safetensors, key components in modern LLMs.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It points to the use of the 'text-generation' pipeline, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels.
The corpus is designed for visual generative modeling and includes 100M training examples captioned by a state-of-the-art VLM.
A key feature is that all images are permissively licensed for both research and commercial use, addressing data accessibility issues.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling an ongoing optimization effort.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm are all teasing Nvidia’s new N1X laptop processors
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Nvidia is reportedly set to announce its own Arm-powered laptop chips at Computex.
The announcement is being heavily teased by Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm.
This signals a significant shift towards custom, potentially AI-optimized, silicon in the PC market.

stories.title: SpaceX gets $4 billion contract to build missile-tracking ‘Golden Dome’ satellites
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: SpaceX secured a substantial $4.16 billion contract from the Pentagon.
The contract involves building missile-tracking satellites for the 'Golden Dome' defense system.
These new sensor-equipped satellites will enable space-based detection and tracking of targets.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the critical, yet often hidden, issue of LLM pretraining data composition ('digital DNA').
The research formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only generated text.
This work enables post-hoc auditing and better understanding of LLM behaviors and failure modes without direct access to proprietary training data.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first LLM designed to generate editable PCB schematics directly from natural language.
Addresses a major industry bottleneck: PCB schematic design is currently manual and highly expertise-dependent.
Overcomes key challenges by developing an LLM-suited representation and addressing the lack of large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current LLM reasoning by decoupling internal computation from external token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition, utilizing an internal 'working memory' mechanism within LLMs.
The goal is to improve the reasoning capabilities of LLMs without relying solely on autoregressive token generation for intermediate steps.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global logical consistency.
Introduces a formal measure, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime computable metric based on system output and coupling constraints to bound compositional incoherence.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current robotics pipelines where motion understanding is often left to downstream policies after static visual encoding.
The framework integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Introduces LLMSurgeon, a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's pretraining data.
Addresses the critical, yet often hidden, issue of LLM 'digital DNA' (pretraining data mixture) influencing model behavior.
Formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to enable post-hoc auditing of LLM data provenance using only generated text.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses a significant industry bottleneck: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic design.
Overcomes key challenges in the field by developing LLM-suited representations and tackling data scarcity.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) for Large Language Models (LLMs), which currently requires slow, per-query adaptation.
It introduces 'HullFT,' a novel method designed to make TTFT practical and fast by optimizing the finetuning process.
The research focuses on balancing the trade-off between fast, potentially redundant retrieval and high-quality, diverse data selection during inference.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS)' to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only generated text.
This work enables post-hoc auditing and analysis of how different data mixtures shape LLM behaviors and failure modes.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first LLM designed to generate editable PCB schematics directly from natural language.
Addresses a significant industry bottleneck: PCB schematic design is currently manual and highly expertise-dependent.
Overcomes key challenges in the field, specifically the lack of an LLM-suited representation and large-scale datasets for this task.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation by providing a benchmark in realistic, structured EHR settings.
Introduces MedCase-Structured, a dataset that generates clinically realistic HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured text.
Enables controllable evaluation of diagnostic reasoning capabilities of LLMs within interoperable clinical data formats.

stories.title: Google Has 1,000 Platform Engineers Making Security Invisible. You Have Zero. Here's How Agents Close the Gap.
stories.author: bala_paranj_059d338e44e7e
bullet_points: Large tech companies (Google, Spotify, Netflix) use internal platforms to enforce security and prevent misconfigurations.
The next evolution involves using autonomous agents to enforce security via reasoning specs against machine-verifiable contracts.
This agent-based approach offers 'multi-cloud abstraction,' meaning the security model is independent of any single cloud provider.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline shown is for text-generation, indicating a focus on large language models.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
The framework includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM usage.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's ability to access current information.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: An AI training startup, Shift, is offering free home cleaning services in New York.
The service requires users to allow the company to film them while performing chores.
Shift plans to expand its service model to other major cities, such as London.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The infrastructure behind the botnet was reportedly linked to a Russia-based residential proxy network.
The news falls under cybersecurity, indicating ongoing threats from organized malicious actors.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude Code, Sonnet, Opus) when supervised by a physicist to develop scientific software (CLAX-PT).
It provides a quantified case study detailing 15 supervision events, classifying how the physicist intervened in the AI's coding process.
The AI demonstrated autonomous problem-solving (10 instances) and utilized the physicist's domain knowledge for further resolution.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: Introduces VideoMLA, a new approach for video diffusion models.
Focuses on optimizing the KV cache by replacing per-head keys and values with a shared low-rank latent attention mechanism (MLA).
Addresses memory and latency issues inherent in long-rollout causal video diffusion models.

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies within sequential time-series data.
Highlights the need for natural-language rationales in anomaly detection, as current benchmarks lack this interpretability.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to facilitate the fine-tuning of VLMs for grounded and interpretable anomaly detection.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base model, can achieve comparable reasoning capabilities to models trained with reinforcement learning.
This method offers a potential alternative to traditional fine-tuning, eliminating the need for extra training, curated datasets, or verifiers.
The practical application of this approach hinges on developing efficient sampling methods to accurately 'mix' into the power distribution.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in Federated Learning (FL) regarding fairness and stability due to fixed aggregation weights.
It introduces the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to accurately reflect unequal and time-varying contributions from participating clients.
This work contributes to making distributed, privacy-sensitive model training more robust and equitable.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of ML research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: the ability to filter poor research proposals before resource-intensive experimentation.
The benchmark is constructed from 1,099 ML research proposals originally submitted to ICLR.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base language model, can elicit comparable reasoning capabilities to models trained with reinforcement learning.
This method offers a potential alternative to traditional fine-tuning, avoiding the need for additional training, curated datasets, or verifiers.
The primary technical challenge identified is developing an efficient sampler capable of accurately drawing samples from this derived power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) where static reward models fail to capture diverse human preferences.
It proposes 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to allow models to generalize to unseen preference domains, overcoming the limitations of fixed multi-reward frameworks.
The research focuses on improving the robustness of preference modeling in LLMs when faced with heterogeneous human values.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in LLM reasoning where intermediate steps are tied to autoregressive token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition to use internal 'working memory' for information manipulation without externalizing thoughts.
The goal is to decouple internal computation from external communication to enhance the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal measure, the compositional residual (eps*), to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
The metric is designed to be computable at runtime using system output and cross-component coupling constraints.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: The research introduces 'COMPOSE' for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The model addresses a gap by requiring generated claims to satisfy both prior work direction and formal structural dependencies.
It aims to overcome limitations in existing methods that only model one of these two crucial constraints.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base language model, can achieve comparable reasoning to models trained with Reinforcement Learning.
This approach bypasses the need for extensive post-training, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The main technical hurdle for practical application is efficiently sampling from this derived power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in LLM evaluation: many existing pairwise rankings fail to meet conventional resolution targets.
Specific findings show unresolved comparisons in major leaderboards like Open LLM Leaderboard v1 and MMLU-Pro.
The research proposes framing paired LLM evaluation as a hypothesis testing problem to improve diagnostic rigor.

stories.title: Show HN: Helios – what plug-in solar could generate for any address in Britain
stories.author: ruaraidh
bullet_points: Helios offers a tool to estimate solar energy generation for specific UK addresses.
The service utilizes UK government LIDAR data to account for local obstructions like buildings and hills.
The product is relevant as plug-in solar panels are becoming legal and available in Britain.

stories.title: How Model Distillation Actually Works (and What the 'China Distilled Our Model' Headlines Really Mean)
stories.author: p0rt
bullet_points: Provides a practical, non-hype technical explanation of knowledge distillation in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Differentiates between standard distillation and the implications of distilling from closed, proprietary APIs.
Addresses and contextualizes recent industry claims regarding model distillation, specifically mentioning OpenAI/Anthropic vs. DeepSeek allegations.

stories.title: Stop Using LLMs to Audit Other LLMs: You Are Bricking Your Production Latency
stories.author: vaxoni
bullet_points: The article warns against using LLMs to audit other LLMs within an AI stack.
The primary concern raised is the negative impact on production latency.
It targets developers working with modern Agentic AI systems.
